INVOCATION - Delivered by: Pastor Jay Gooding, Senior Pastor, Miracle Revival Fellowship Tabernacle Church, 155 McCombs Road, Bronx, NY 10453.

Motion to spread the Invocation in full upon the record by Council Member Aldebol.

The Invocation was delivered by Pastor Jay Gooding, Senior Pastor, Miracle Revival Fellowship Tabernacle Church, 155 McCombs Road, Bronx, N.Y. 10453.

Council Member Aldebol moved to spread the Invocation in full upon the Record.

Items.MinutesNote: At this point, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member ______) declared that the aforementioned items were adopted and referred to the Committee on Land Use and to the appropriate Land Use Subcommittee.

Items.Title:
By Council Member Marte:
Pursuant to Rule 11.20(b) of the Council Rules and Section 19-160.2 of the New York City Administrative Code, the Council resolves that the action of the Department of Transportation approving a sidewalk café located at 37 Canal Street, New York, NY 10002 Borough of Manhattan, Council District 1, Community District 3, related to Application No. D 2650119000 SWM (Le Dive) shall be subject to review by the Council.
Items.ActionText: A motion was made that this Land Use Call-Up be Approved, by Council approved by Roll Call.
Items.MatterName: Zoning, Le Dive, Manhattan (D 2650119000 SWM).

Items.Title:
Introduction & Reading of Bills
See Attached
Discussion of Resolutions
Resolutions
General Discussion
Extension of Remarks

INTRODUCTION AND READING OF BILLS

Pursuant to Section 33-a of the New York City Charter, the New York City Council may vote on the following items no earlier than 30 days from the date of this notice. The New York City Council reserves the right to vote without the notice required pursuant to Section 33-a on any proposed local laws that do not relate to the public safety operations of the New York City Police Department, the Fire Department or the Department of Correction.
Items.AgendaNote: INTRODUCTION AND READING OF BILLS - Pursuant to Section 33-a of the New York City Charter, the New York City Council may vote on the following items no earlier than 30 days from the date of this notice. The New York City Council reserves the right to vote without the notice required pursuant to Section 33-a on any proposed local laws that do not relate to the public safety operations of the New York City Police Department, the Fire Department or the Department of Correction.

Items.MinutesNote: The Majority Leader (Council Member Abreu) presiding as the President Pro Tempore.

The presence of a quorum was announced by the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu).

There is one vacant seat on the Council pending the swearing-in of the certified winner of a Special Election to be held on April 28, 2026 in the 3rd Council District (Manhattan).

There were 49 Council Members marked present at this Stated Meeting.

The Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member ______) put the question whether the Council would agree with and adopt such motion which was decided in the affirmative by the following vote:

At this point, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) declared that the aforementioned items were adopted and referred to the Committee on Land Use and to the appropriate Land Use Subcommittee.
